campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they remembered K9 Lucas, a police dog who recently passed away. The council also moved forward on several items including an updated beekeeping rule, a study about homeless services in the West Valley region, and discussed a state bill about transit-oriented development (though several council members had to step aside due to potential conflicts of interest).
keyTopics: Memorial for K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
West Valley Homeless Services Feasibility Study recommending a regional task force
Senate Bill 79 (Transit-Oriented Development) public hearing with council member recusals
Fiscal Year 2025 financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a special meeting to review and approve the upcoming year's plans for five different city commissions that handle everything from parks and libraries to traffic safety and planning. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for 2026-27, allowing the council to align these plans with the city's broader direction. The council also briefly discussed a legal matter in a private session.
keyTopics: Parks and Recreation Commission's priorities for 2026-27
Heritage Preservation Commission plans and historic preservation goals
Library and Public Art Commission initiatives
Traffic Safety Commission's work plan for improving road safety
Planning Commission's development and zoning priorities

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes, reviewing the city's finances, and completing the weed abatement program. The council also adopted new procedures for how they operate, approved raising the Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags, and discussed housing and transportation planning.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program wrapped up for 2026
New rules established for how City Council conducts meetings
Below Market Rate housing unit transfer exemption adopted
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags approved for raising
VTA transportation presentation and housing element implementation planning

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and check progress on housing and general planning goals. The council also approved a new residential development (Solana Project) and finalized plans for community health and senior services in 2026.
keyTopics: January 2026 city finances and investment report
Solana Project residential development approved
Housing Element and General Plan progress update
2026 Community Health and Senior Services programs and budget
Updated meeting minute procedures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to organize and prepare for upcoming council meetings scheduled for April 7 and April 14. The council reviewed which items should be added to the agendas, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked whether proper public notice requirements were met for any agenda items or documents.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7 council meeting agenda
Prepared the draft agenda for the April 14 council meeting
Set time limits for specific agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Verified public notice requirements were followed for all agenda items
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments from residents. The council also reviewed committee reports and set upcoming meeting dates for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comments and questions from residents
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The group approved meeting notes from their previous session and heard presentations from organizations asking for funding to help residents with housing and support services.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed
Community development funding decisions
Human services program proposals
Meeting minutes approval from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching several new programs. They discussed a business license amnesty program to help shops get compliant with city rules, launched a 'Shop Local' summer campaign to boost spending at Cupertino stores, and explored ways to partner with private companies to sponsor city events and support small businesses and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping non-compliant shops get legal without penalties
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign - promoting spending at local Cupertino businesses
Public-Private Partnerships for City Events - getting private companies to help fund and sponsor city activities
Economic Incentives for Retail and Startups - new programs to attract and support small businesses
Shopping Center Outreach - direct engagement with mall and strip center tenants

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026 to review matters related to the city's historic buildings and landmarks. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom, and had the option to submit written comments by email before the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom participation)
Written public comment submissions accepted via email
Historic preservation and landmark review decisions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative tasks like approving previous meeting notes and scheduling future meetings. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to a 2026 wellness conference and confirmed a new representative to work on economic development issues with the school district.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming meeting dates
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
New Milpitas Unified School District representative confirmed for Economic Development committee
City Manager given authority to negotiate contracts (details incomplete in agenda)

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two important agreements. The council approved a disaster relief partnership with the American Red Cross to use city facilities when emergencies occur, and authorized spending about $93,400 per year for three years on email security software (Mimecast) to protect the city's computer systems.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report items added to agenda
